summary: Preserves the selected source logical coordinate interval in every view/copy slice.
two-bound .. or ..< slice; static diagnostics and dynamic IndexError precede view creation
requires: the enclosing IndexSuffix, not expression Pratt, owns the slice delimiter
the receiver logical domain and optional bounds are exact
bounds evaluate once left-to-right before selection
preconditions: the delimiter is .. or ..< and both bounds are present
^ and $ occur only as SliceBound anchors; * is the only full-axis spelling
the selected receiver domain has ordered closed bounds
normalization: preserve delimiter spelling, normalize ^/$ anchors against the receiver's first/last logical coordinates, and retain source-domain bounds without rebasing
decision_procedure: reject .../..> or either missing bound with the exact range/empty-range diagnostic; ^/$ express endpoints
evaluate lower then upper once; reject statically out-of-domain or reversed bounds
a dynamic bounds failure raises IndexError::outOfLogicalDomain before any view or ownership commit
.. admits inclusive coordinates; ..< admits lower..upper-1 with SLICE_HALF_OPEN_RANGE_NONCANONICAL; never infer step/rebase
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: a slice result is a readonly view tied to one live owner and retains the selected source logical coordinates; hidden copy, rebase, mutation, escape, and isolation crossing are forbidden
requires: the source owner identity and live borrow region are exact
the ordered selected logical coordinates and result-view provenance are preserved
the operation requests only a readonly view
preconditions: the view has exactly one source owner and a bounded borrow region
selected coordinates are expressed in the source logical domain
no implicit copy, rebase, mutable assignment, escape, or isolation crossing has been requested
normalization: normalize owner identity, borrow region, isolation, ordered source coordinates and view provenance while preserving the source logical-domain labels
decision_procedure: reject any requested or observed coordinate rebase with SLICE_LOGICAL_DOMAIN_REBASE_FORBIDDEN
reject mutable slice assignment with SLICE_MUTABLE_ASSIGNMENT_UNSUPPORTED; a readonly view exposes no write place
reject a view whose borrow region can outlive its owner with SLICE_VIEW_ESCAPES_OWNER
reject an isolation crossing without an explicit owned copy with SLICE_VIEW_CROSSES_ISOLATION_WITHOUT_OWNED_COPY
admit only the owner-bounded readonly view with unchanged coordinates and no hidden copy
create no product execution receipt
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: ordered branch 1: normalize every manifest path to one project-root-relative lexical key before parsing; normalized_path_conflict true means the first repeated key emits SOURCE_ROLE_CARRIER_CONFLICT even when both rows name the same role; ordered branch 2: the manifest role is authoritative; source_role or an external CLI, API, or IDE role unequal to manifest_source_role emits SOURCE_ROLE_CARRIER_CONFLICT; ordered branch 3: a library target containing any script-classified source root emits LIBRARY_TARGET_CONTAINS_TOP_LEVEL_SCRIPT; ordered branch 4: an explicit entry in a library emits ENTRY_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_LIBRARY_SOURCE; a top-level statement in a library or executable root emits TOP_LEVEL_STATEMENT_REQUIRES_SCRIPT_ROOT; an executable top-level binding emits TOP_LEVEL_BINDING_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_EXECUTABLE_SOURCE; an explicit entry in a script emits SCRIPT_ROOT_AND_ENTRY_DECL_CONFLICT; ordered branch 5: explicit_entry_count unequal to selected_entry_target_count emits SOURCE_ROLE_ENTRY_COUNT_MISMATCH; after equality, executable zero/multiple counts emit NO_EXECUTABLE_ENTRY/ENTRY_DECL_DUPLICATE and library/script counts must both be zero; only after all branches pass does source_role choose exactly one Stable or Preview role root; trying roots to infer a role is forbidden
requires: ordered branch 1: normalize every manifest path to one project-root-relative lexical key before parsing; normalized_path_conflict true means the first repeated key emits SOURCE_ROLE_CARRIER_CONFLICT even when both rows name the same role
ordered branch 2: the manifest role is authoritative; source_role or an external CLI, API, or IDE role unequal to manifest_source_role emits SOURCE_ROLE_CARRIER_CONFLICT
ordered branch 3: a library target containing any script-classified source root emits LIBRARY_TARGET_CONTAINS_TOP_LEVEL_SCRIPT

ordered branch 4: an explicit entry in a library emits ENTRY_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_LIBRARY_SOURCE; a top-level statement in a library or executable root emits TOP_LEVEL_STATEMENT_REQUIRES_SCRIPT_ROOT; an executable top-level binding emits TOP_LEVEL_BINDING_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_EXECUTABLE_SOURCE; an explicit entry in a script emits SCRIPT_ROOT_AND_ENTRY_DECL_CONFLICT

ordered branch 5: explicit_entry_count unequal to selected_entry_target_count emits SOURCE_ROLE_ENTRY_COUNT_MISMATCH; after equality, executable zero/multiple counts emit NO_EXECUTABLE_ENTRY/ENTRY_DECL_DUPLICATE and library/script counts must both be zero
only after all branches pass does source_role choose exactly one Stable or Preview role root; trying roots to infer a role is forbidden
preconditions: ordered branch 1: normalize every manifest path to one project-root-relative lexical key before parsing; normalized_path_conflict true means the first repeated key emits SOURCE_ROLE_CARRIER_CONFLICT even when both rows name the same role
ordered branch 2: the manifest role is authoritative; source_role or an external CLI, API, or IDE role unequal to manifest_source_role emits SOURCE_ROLE_CARRIER_CONFLICT
ordered branch 3: a library target containing any script-classified source root emits LIBRARY_TARGET_CONTAINS_TOP_LEVEL_SCRIPT

ordered branch 4: an explicit entry in a library emits ENTRY_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_LIBRARY_SOURCE; a top-level statement in a library or executable root emits TOP_LEVEL_STATEMENT_REQUIRES_SCRIPT_ROOT; an executable top-level binding emits TOP_LEVEL_BINDING_NOT_ALLOWED_IN_EXECUTABLE_SOURCE; an explicit entry in a script emits SCRIPT_ROOT_AND_ENTRY_DECL_CONFLICT

ordered branch 5: explicit_entry_count unequal to selected_entry_target_count emits SOURCE_ROLE_ENTRY_COUNT_MISMATCH; after equality, executable zero/multiple counts emit NO_EXECUTABLE_ENTRY/ENTRY_DECL_DUPLICATE and library/script counts must both be zero
only after all branches pass does source_role choose exactly one Stable or Preview role root; trying roots to infer a role is forbidden
normalization: normalize aliases, ownership regions, rows, labels, and witness identities before decision
decision_procedure: validate the closed pre-V5 descriptor and pre-V5-INV-SOURCE-ROLE-CARRIER
normalize project paths and carrier roles before parsing any source content
evaluate diagnostic_dispatch branches 1 through 6 in numeric order and emit only the first matching diagnostic
after every branch passes, select exactly the declared role root without trial parsing and return SourceRoleAdmitted
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic
